summary: This is a lecture on Islamic beliefs and superstitions, discussing how pre-Islamic Arabs engaged in practices like drawing lots at the Kaaba to make decisions, believing in unlucky months and astrology. The speaker explains how Shaitan influences people by making them focus on rare correct predictions while ignoring numerous failures, and how these superstitions can resurface in Islamic dress, such as randomly opening the Quran to seek guidance. The second part transitions to discussing the opening chapter of the Quran (Surah Al-Fatiha), emphasizing the names of Allah—particularly Al-Rahman (The Merciful)—and the boundless mercy of Allah, illustrated through hadith narrations about divine compassion that far exceeds human maternal love.
suggested_title: Eradicating Superstitions and Understanding Allah's Mercy: Surah Al-Fatiha Tafsir
hadith_chapter: Book of Faith (Eman), various chapters on mercy and divine attributes
catchy_title: Why Muslims Still Fall for Superstition
